name: rasa.module.http
description: Generic HTTP/REST access for agents — a portable zero-dependency MCP server (http_request, http_get_json, http_paginate, http_capabilities) with deny-by-default host allowlisting, env-injected auth, truncation-honest envelopes and per-call audit lines, plus the unified connection.json spec templates and the /http and /api-onboard skills. The rung-1 opener of the Connections track (canon SA-020/SA-031): works today in any consumer project via .mcp.json, and in kernel turns via TASK-171. Typed connector Elements through the SA-031 kernel choke point are the durable path — this module carries a written sunset to dev-utility once SA-031 lands.

rasa.note: The Connections-track rung-1 module. Honesty tier stated plainly: allowlist + redaction enforcement live INSIDE the shipped tool process (self-policing) — the enforced tier is SA-031's kernel-side HttpExecutor (call-time vault resolution, kernel-enforced allowlist, per-call audit events), at which point this module sunsets to dev-utility. Secrets discipline: names in specs, values ONLY in env / the kernel vault; the /api-onboard wizard is SCAFFOLD-ONLY and never accepts credential values in chat. The connection.json templates are a strict subset of the SA-031 schema (field names LOCKED there; divergence is drift and SA-031 wins). Cross-refs to canon tasks SA-020/SA-031 and kernel TASK-171 are SOFT — the module functions standalone in any Claude Code project.

element.description: Element-owned files mirrored into the install target's .claude/ per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Refreshed on Element upgrade. The consumer's live connection specs are NOT here — they are project-owned under .claude/connections/ (see seed.files).

files.note: The portable HTTP spine: honesty tier + sunset, secrets rules (vault/env only, never chat/committed/logged), deny-by-default allowlist, write-op discipline, the locked unified connection-spec shape, truncation awareness, the error-code table, audit lines. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.

HTTP skills: /http (call external APIs properly — capabilities-first, envelope handling, missing_auth stop rule, write-op confirmation) and /api-onboard (scaffold a connection spec + per-API SKILL.md; SCAFFOLD-ONLY, never accepts tokens in chat). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.

The zero-dependency stdio MCP server (mcp-http-server.mjs, Node >= 18) + its reference README. Wire into the project via the mcp-config snippet in templates/ — the server runs from .claude/http/mcp-http-server.mjs. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.

The unified connection-spec templates (connection.rest.json.template, connection.mcp.json.template — strict subset of the SA-031 schema), the per-API SKILL.md 9-section template (api-skill.md.template), and the .mcp.json wiring snippet (mcp-config.snippet.json). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.
Describes what this module ships and how it installs. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

seed.description: One-time, project-owned files copied at install only (skip-if-exists), per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. The consumer's connection specs live under .claude/connections/ and belong to the project — never overwritten on Element upgrade.

files.note: Seeds the project-owned connections folder: one folder per connection (connection.json + SKILL.md), the secrets rule, the /api-onboard pointer, and the promotion path to SA-031 connector Elements.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). When co-installed under a parent that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path, Lock L-004). Substitutes the standard {{ELEMENT_*}} / {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer. The connections/ folder ships as a seeded README instead (bin/init does not implement scaffold.directories), so this stays empty.
